Function Plotter
  • Home
  • About

On this page

  • Inputs
  • Plot
  • Current function

Function Plotter

Code 
math = require("https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/mathjs@12.4.1/lib/browser/math.js")

Enter a function of (x), choose a plotting range, and the graph updates automatically.

Examples:

sin(x)
cos(x)
x^2
exp(-x^2)
sin(x)/x
sqrt(abs(x))

Inputs

Code 
viewof funcText = Inputs.text({
  label: "Function f(x)",
  value: "sin(x)",
  placeholder: "e.g. sin(x), x^2, exp(-x^2)"
})

viewof xmin = Inputs.number({
  label: "x minimum",
  value: -10,
  step: 0.1
})

viewof xmax = Inputs.number({
  label: "x maximum",
  value: 10,
  step: 0.1
})

viewof npoints = Inputs.range([100, 3000], {
  label: "Number of points",
  value: 1000,
  step: 100
})

viewof autoY = Inputs.toggle({
  label: "Auto y-axis",
  value: true
})

viewof ymin = Inputs.number({
  label: "Manual y minimum",
  value: -2,
  step: 0.1
})

viewof ymax = Inputs.number({
  label: "Manual y maximum",
  value: 2,
  step: 0.1
})

Plot

Code 
compiledFunction = {
  try {
    return math.compile(funcText);
  } catch (error) {
    return null;
  }
}
Code 
errorMessage = {
  try {
    math.compile(funcText);
    return "";
  } catch (error) {
    return error.message;
  }
}
Code 
html`<p style="color:red;">${errorMessage}</p>`
Code 
data = {
  const points = [];

  if (!compiledFunction || xmax <= xmin) {
    return points;
  }

  const dx = (xmax - xmin) / (npoints - 1);

  for (let i = 0; i < npoints; i++) {
    const x = xmin + i * dx;

    let y;
    try {
      y = compiledFunction.evaluate({x});
    } catch (error) {
      y = NaN;
    }

    if (Number.isFinite(y)) {
      points.push({x, y});
    }
  }

  return points;
}
Code 
yDomain = {
  if (autoY || data.length === 0) {
    return undefined;
  } else {
    return [ymin, ymax];
  }
}
Code 
Plot.plot({
  width: 750,
  height: 450,
  grid: true,
  x: {
    label: "x",
    domain: [xmin, xmax]
  },
  y: {
    label: "f(x)",
    domain: yDomain
  },
  marks: [
    Plot.line(data, {
      x: "x",
      y: "y",
      stroke: "steelblue",
      strokeWidth: 2
    }),
    Plot.ruleY([0], {stroke: "black", strokeOpacity: 0.4}),
    Plot.ruleX([0], {stroke: "black", strokeOpacity: 0.4})
  ]
})

Current function

Code 
html`<p>You are plotting: <b>f(x) = ${funcText}</b></p>`
